---
title: ""
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

## Probability & ML Foundations

Statistics and ML from the ground up — each topic opened with a business problem you've already faced. For internal auditors, financial analysts, and fraud examiners. **April – July 2026.**

::: {.content-list}

::: {.content-item}
::: {.content-item-meta}
WEEK 1 · APR 13–19 · [Live]{.badge-live}
:::
**[Probability Conditioning & Bayes](content/en/week-01-conditioning.qmd)**

Given a $10K invoice from a new vendor — what's the real fraud probability? Conditional probability · Bayes' theorem · Law of total probability.
:::

::: {.content-item .content-item-upcoming}
::: {.content-item-meta}
WEEK 2 · APR 20–26
:::
**Distributions & Uncertainty**

How to model outcomes you can't observe directly.
:::

::: {.content-item .content-item-upcoming}
::: {.content-item-meta}
WEEK 3 · APR 27 – MAY 3
:::
**Maximum Likelihood & Estimation**

The principle behind most ML models you already use.
:::

::: {.content-item .content-item-upcoming}
::: {.content-item-meta}
WEEK 4–12 · MAY – JUL
:::
**Hypothesis Testing · Regression · Classification · Clustering · and more**

Full syllabus on the [Series page](projects.qmd).
:::

:::

*New lessons published weekly. [View all projects →](projects.qmd)*
